context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=2; meal=bite-sized sandwiches; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose front-occlusion minimization in a front-facing social context, indicating sensitivity to blocking the forward view. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the user's right THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user chose right-occlusion minimization while watching TV on the right, indicating that occlusion preferences track the location of the valued visual target.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a faster-than-default speed in that context, suggesting some willingness to trade maximum caution for efficiency during that activity.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food acquisition must remain compatible with limited mouth opening THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and has consistently selected the narrower-bite option.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF the user's physical constraints are relevant to the meal context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer less compatible with their abilities.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside-mouth transfer. IF the user's physical constraints are considered for food delivery THEN not applicable BECAUSE the observed preference and physical profile support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF initiating feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging.

IF initiating drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging.

IF initiating mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF completing feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected the reliable perception-based option and this avoids requiring extra physical input.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF completing drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based drinking completion less suitable.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF completing mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based wiping completion less suitable.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN yes BECAUSE the user chose retraction in a front-social context, consistent with wanting to reduce prolonged visual obstruction. IF dining context involves a focal activity to the user's right THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to retract while watching TV on the right, suggesting that side-located visual activities do not by themselves trigger a preference for added retraction time.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social THEN less BECAUSE the user chose reduced dipping with sauce in a social context, suggesting a preference to limit messiness around others. IF food does not include a dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant and the user selected no dipping for a non-dippable meal.
